evidences.content: The game kicked off with high intensity, and Monaco quickly found the back of the net. Takumi Minamino opened the scoring in the 5th minute with a precise right-footed strike from the middle of the penalty area, assisted by Maghnes Akliouche. However, Pafos responded swiftly, with David Luiz equalizing with a powerful header from a corner kick in the 18th minute, assisted by Mislav Orsic. Monaco regained the lead in the 26th minute through Folarin Balogun, who executed a left-footed strike from the middle of the penalty area. The match then settled into a tense midfield battle, with both sides creating chances. evidences.title: Preview: Pafos FC vs Monaco - prediction, team news, lineups
evidences.content: On matchday five, Pafos will be without Pedrao, who is recovering from cruciate ligament surgery, while Joao Correia will miss another match through suspension. Derrick Luckassen scored early in the second half against Villareal, while Neofytos Michail made five stops for his third clean sheet of the League Phase. A hamstring strain could keep Christian Mawissa out of the fold for Les Monegasques, while Eric Dier suffered a setback in his recovery from a calf issue and Paul Pogba played his first minutes of football on Saturday since his doping ban over two years ago. [...] Meanwhile, they enter this contest on a three-match unbeaten run in this tournament and have an opportunity to win successive Champions League away games in the group phase for the first time in the 21st century. Two of their previous three competitive victories took place outside Stade Louis II, with Les Monegasques collecting points in three of their last four matches played away from home. However, both teams have shown they can compete against strong opposition, with Pafos holding Villarreal to a 1-0 win and Monaco drawing against Tottenham and Manchester City. evidences.title: Pafos FC 2-2 Monaco: Post-match review, Stats, Key moments - Bwin
evidences.content: # Pafos FC 2-2 Monaco: Post-match review, Stats, Key moments Published: Dive into Bwin’s post-match review of Pafos FC 2-2 Monaco in the Champions League on Wednesday, 26 November 2025, with stats, key moments and our take on the match. Pafos FC vs Monaco, bwin ## Pafos FC 2-2 Monaco Post-Match Review Monaco struck early in this Europa League encounter, with Takumi Minamino finding the net in the 5th minute to give the visitors a swift lead. Pafos FC responded well, equalising through David Luiz in the 18th minute from a Mislav Orsic assist, but Monaco regained the advantage when Folarin Balogun scored in the 26th. The first half saw several yellow cards, including one for Pafos’s David Goldar in the 45+3rd minute, setting a tense tone as both sides pushed for control.
